blurb_en: ElevenLabs did everything right — licensed consent, explicit project approval, royalties on every play — and the product is a 13-hour AI audiobook of Homer's Odyssey narrated by a voice replica of Michael Caine. Caine licensed his voice and likeness to ElevenLabs last year; he was consulted specifically on this project and approved the marketing materials. The full cast is AI voices, 20 characters, with an AI-generated score; six weeks to produce. Christopher Nolan's film adaptation opens July 17, which is less coincidence than it is a marketing calendar. Homer died roughly 2,700 years ago and gets no royalties — the first unconsented voice in the production.
blurb_zh: ElevenLabs本周发布了一版《奥德赛》有声书，长达13小时，由迈克尔·凯恩的AI声音复刻担任旁白——一切程序合规：去年已签授权协议，本次项目获凯恩本人明确同意，每次播放均支付版权费。全剧共20个角色，均为AI配音，配乐同样由AI生成，前后历时6周制作完成。诺兰改编的同名电影7月17日上映，这个时间点与其说是巧合，不如说是营销日历。荷马本人已逝世约两千七百年，版权费一分未收——是这部作品里唯一未经授权的声音。
verification_notes: Confirmed via Variety (June 23), Deadline, and Hollywood Reporter. All three entertainment trades confirm the audiobook release, Caine's licensed voice, and the Nolan film tie-in. ElevenReader (ElevenLabs app) is the distribution platform.

blurb_en: Google DeepMind invested $75 million in A24 Monday for a big picture the rest of Hollywood is watching: how to work with AI without handing over your content catalog. DeepMind researchers will work alongside A24 to build AI tools for production workflows — storyboarding, shot planning, and post-production automation — while A24's library remains off the table. A24 is the last major studio to formalize a Hollywood-AI deal; Netflix, Amazon Studios, and Lionsgate (which took an equity stake in Runway) have had active partnerships for months. For Google: production workflow data without the copyright exposure of training on a studio catalog. For A24: $75 million and a DeepMind research team.
blurb_zh: 谷歌DeepMind本周向A24注资7500万美元，换取与这家独立电影公司的AI研究合作——但不包括A24内容库或版权数据的访问权。DeepMind研究人员将驻场协助开发AI制作工具，涵盖故事板、分镜规划和后期自动化流程。A24是好莱坞最后一家正式签下AI合作协议的主要制片公司，奈飞、亚马逊影业和狮门（持有Runway股权）早已到位。谷歌获得制作工作流数据，规避了在版权片库训练AI的法律风险；A24获得DeepMind研究团队和7500万美元。
verification_notes: Confirmed via Variety, TechCrunch, Deadline, and Hollywood Reporter (all June 22). Google DeepMind official blog also confirms the partnership. No content library access confirmed explicitly by multiple sources. $75M investment amount confirmed.

blurb_en: ByteDance gave creators a new frame of reference today: Seedance 2.5 accepts 50 simultaneous inputs — images, video, audio, and 3D models combined — and generates native 30-second clips. A 3D white-box preview function ships alongside it, letting users see a wireframe sketch of the shot before committing compute to a full render; ByteDance says it is the first video model to offer this. Seedance 2.0 is also being upgraded to 4K in parallel. Seedream 5.0, a new image model announced on the same stage, adds deep thinking and built-in online search — images tied to current events without manual prompt engineering. Both models are in enterprise beta; public launch is early July.
blurb_zh: 字节跳动今日在火山引擎FORCE大会发布Seedance 2.5，支持最多50路多模态参考输入（图片、视频、音频、3D模型均可组合），原生输出30秒视频。同步推出3D线框预览功能，可在正式生成前查看镜头构图草图，字节称在视频生成领域尚属首创。Seedance 2.0同步升级4K输出。同台亮相的还有Seedream 5.0图像模型，集成深度推理与在线检索能力，生成图像时可直接引用时事内容。两款模型目前处于企业测试阶段，预计7月初公开上线。
verification_notes: Confirmed via The Information briefing (June 23) and BigGo Finance citing official ByteDance announcement. Volcano Engine FORCE conference June 23 confirmed. 30-second native clips, 50 reference inputs, 3D white-box preview, 4K upgrade for 2.0, and Seedream 5.0 with online search all confirmed across multiple sources including aibase.com.

blurb_en: Nvidia ran a shell game on Groq in December: paid $20 billion for a non-exclusive license to their inference technology, hired founder Jonathan Ross and president Sunny Madra, and left the company standing — just without anyone running it. Nvidia shipped its own Groq-derived LPX inference system in March. On Monday, the remaining Groq announced a $650 million raise with an entirely new leadership team — new COO from xAI, new CTO, new CPO — and pivoted to its cloud inference business. The transaction is being called a 'not-acqui-hire': take the patents and the people, leave the shell. The shell just raised $650 million.
blurb_zh: 英伟达去年12月向Groq支付约200亿美元——不是收购，而是技术非独家授权，加上挖走创始人乔纳森·罗斯和总裁桑尼·马德拉，留下了一个空壳。英伟达在今年3月推出了自己的LPX推理系统。Groq于6月22日宣布6.5亿美元新融资，配备全新管理层——新COO来自xAI，另配新CTO和CPO，转型云推理业务。业内将这种操作称为not-acqui-hire：把人和专利都拿走，但让企业外壳继续存活。这个外壳刚刚融了6.5亿美元。
verification_notes: Confirmed via TechCrunch (June 22) and Yahoo Finance. $20B Nvidia December license deal confirmed. $650M raise confirmed. New leadership including COO from xAI confirmed. 'Not-acqui-hire' framing confirmed across multiple outlets.
